title: drain_tx_queue re-queues packets with wrong priority after CAD failure
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:354

When CAD fails in drain_tx_queue(), the popped packet is re-queued with hardcoded Priority.ROUTING regardless of its original priority:

    self.tx_queue.push(frame_bytes, priority=Priority.ROUTING)

This causes priority escalation: a BULK packet that was legitimately lowest-priority becomes highest-priority after CAD failure. Subsequent legitimate ROUTING packets could be preempted by this escalated packet.

Example scenario:
1. Queue has one BULK packet
2. drain_tx_queue() pops it, CAD fails
3. Packet re-queued as ROUTING
4. New ROUTING packet arrives
5. Escalated BULK packet transmits before legitimate ROUTING packet (wrong)

The comment says 'Use ROUTING priority to ensure it goes back to front' but this violates priority semantics. The fix should preserve the original priority, which requires either:
- Changing pop() to return TxQueueEntry instead of just bytes
- Using a separate re-queue method that doesn't expire stale entries
- Tracking metadata alongside frame_bytes
